nodes.label: Gen 6.1 Compute FPGA-STP Server 3U (C2310)
facts: 6 Storm Peak FPGA cards with 4 DIMMs each
Longs Peak FPGA with integrated Mellanox ConnectX-4 NIC
PCIe risers with PLX switches for FPGA connectivity

components.name: FPGA Memory 16GB RDIMM (24x, 4 per Storm Peak)
Longs Peak FPGA Accelerator (onboard Mellanox ConnectX-4)
FPGA Memory 16GB RDIMM (24x, 4 per Storm Peak)
Longs Peak FPGA Card A2010 (onboard Mellanox ConnectX-4 LX 50GbE)
3-Slot Active x32 Riser + PCIe Switch (PEX/PLX domain)

symptoms.title: Node in D State / Unidentified Issue
checks: Collect BMC event logs and sensor snapshot (ipmitool sel list / ipmitool sensor / BMC UI export). Look for power, thermal, or fatal platform errors.
Record BIOS/BMC firmware versions (sh system info -i <slot> / ipmiutil fru / SMTK system info). Verify against known-good baseline.
Collect PCIe device presence summary (lspci / SMTK hardware inventory / BIOS device inventory). Check: NIC present? FPGA cards present? NVMe controllers present?

evidence.description: BMC event logs and sensor snapshot
PCIe device presence summary (NIC / FPGA / NVMe present or missing)

symptoms.title: Blade Is Not Visible in Rack Manager
checks: Verify blade appears in Rack Manager (RM) UI or CLI
Check management cable connection between blade and RM (reseat if needed)
Verify RM port assignment is correct for this blade slot
Check PSU status LEDs � verify all PSUs are powered and healthy
Verify PDB/PMDU power delivery path to this slot
If blade appears after above checks, pivot to NODE_D_STATE for further triage

symptoms.title: BMC Unresponsive / Cannot Connect to BMC
checks: Verify BMC network connectivity (ping BMC IP)
Check management cable connection and port assignment
Try BMC reset via hardware button or RM command
Collect BMC event logs and sensor snapshot if accessible (ipmitool sel list / ipmitool sensor)
Record BIOS/BMC firmware versions if accessible
Collect PCIe device presence summary if OS is accessible

symptoms.title: Power Issue / PSU / PDU / CMOS
checks: Check all PSU status LEDs and BMC power sensor readings
Verify PSU PMBus cable routing (confirm PSU1/2/3 cables not inverted)
Check PDU/PMDU breaker status for this slot
Verify CMOS battery voltage if BIOS settings reset unexpectedly
Reseat suspect PSU and verify BMC logs the event
Try known-good PSU if available
Check for correlated thermal events in BMC logs

evidence.description: Photos of PSU LEDs and PMBus cable routing
List of parts swapped and results

symptoms.title: Motherboard / Board Replacement
checks: Confirm motherboard replacement is warranted (min boot config tried, other components ruled out)
Record current BIOS/BMC firmware versions before replacement
Document all current BIOS settings (boot order, PCIe config)
After replacement: update BIOS/BMC to fleet-standard firmware
After replacement: reconfigure BIOS settings (boot order, PCIe)
After replacement: verify TPM/Cerberus re-enrollment if applicable
After replacement: run full validation (PXE, FPGA enum, NVMe, stress test)

symptoms.title: BIOS / BMC Firmware Issue
checks: Record current BIOS and BMC firmware versions
Compare against fleet-standard / known-good firmware baseline
Check for known firmware bugs matching observed symptoms
Plan firmware update if mismatch found
After update: verify BIOS settings did not reset to defaults
After update: verify boot order and PCIe configuration

symptoms.title: Multiple Storm Peak FPGAs Missing / Not Detected
checks: Check shared-domain issues FIRST: riser/PLX/switch/power/active riser before swapping individual FPGA cards
Verify all power supplies are healthy and providing adequate power
Inspect PCIe riser seating and PLX switch status LEDs
Check for common power rail issues affecting multiple slots
Verify active riser PCIe switch initialization in BIOS/POST
Reseat risers before reseating individual FPGA cards
Verify PSU PMBus cable routing (confirm PSU1/2/3 cables not inverted)
Check FPGA slot power indicators
Reseat Oculink cable(s) - verify not pinched or damaged
Try known-good FPGA if available (late-stage)

evidence.description: BIOS POST codes and error messages
Photos of riser LED status indicators
Photos of PSU LEDs and PMBus cable routing
List of parts swapped and results of each swap

symptoms.title: PCIe Riser Card Issue
checks: Inspect riser card seating in motherboard slot
Check PLX switch status LEDs on active riser
Verify all devices on this riser are missing (shared-domain pattern)
Reseat the riser card
Check for bent pins or physical damage on riser connector
Try the suspect FPGA card in a different riser slot (if available)
Try known-good riser if available

evidence.description: BIOS POST codes and error messages
Photos of riser LED status indicators
List of parts swapped and results

symptoms.title: Oculink Cable Issue
checks: Inspect Oculink cable for physical damage or pinching
Verify both ends of the Oculink cable are fully seated
Check cable routing path � ensure no sharp bends or pinch points
Reseat the Oculink cable at both ends
Try known-good Oculink cable if available
Verify Longs Peak FPGA LEDs after cable reseat

evidence.description: Longs Peak FPGA LED status after reseat
lspci output before and after cable reseat

symptoms.title: PXE Boot Timeout / Network Boot Failure
checks: Verify Longs Peak FPGA LEDs / health status
Verify Oculink cable seated and not pinched
Validate Mellanox NIC config/firmware and PXE enabled
Collect serial console logs during PXE attempt
Check BIOS boot order includes PXE
Verify network switch port configuration
Check ToR switch port assignment matches expected blade port (DHCP lease state)
Verify DAC cable integrity and seating
Try known-good NIC if available
Attempt minimum boot config to isolate (late-stage)

evidence.description: Switch port status and VLAN config
ToR switch port config and DHCP lease state
Photos of PSU LEDs and cabling

symptoms.title: Network Interface Down / Link Failure
checks: Check Longs Peak FPGA status and link LEDs
Verify Oculink cable integrity and seating
Verify DAC cable integrity and seating
Check switch port status and error counters
Verify NIC driver loaded correctly
Check for firmware mismatch
Try known-good NIC if available (late-stage)

evidence.description: dmesg or Event Viewer network errors
ToR switch port config and DHCP lease state

symptoms.title: DAC Cable Issue / Miswire / Loose Cable
checks: Inspect DAC cable for physical damage
Verify DAC cable is seated at both ends (NIC side and ToR side)
Verify correct port mapping (blade port to ToR port)
Check ToR switch port status and error counters for the connected port
Try known-good DAC cable if available
After replacement: verify link comes up and PXE/network works

evidence.description: ToR switch port status and error counters

symptoms.title: NVMe SSD Not Detected / Missing
checks: Verify M.2 NVMe SSD seated correctly
Check BIOS storage controller settings
Verify NVMe driver loaded
Check for thermal throttling or power issues
Inspect M.2 slot for damage

evidence.description: BMC sensor readings for storage temps

symptoms.title: CPU Issue / CPU Replacement
checks: Check BMC event logs for CPU-related errors (IERR, CATERR, machine check)
Verify CPU thermal readings are within spec
Check for BIOS POST errors related to CPU
If replacing: document current BIOS settings before swap
After replacement: expect PXE timeout on first boot (memory re-training)
After replacement: verify BIOS settings did not reset

symptoms.title: Memory DIMM Issue / DIMM Replacement
checks: Check BMC event logs for memory errors (correctable/uncorrectable ECC)
Verify DIMM population matches expected configuration
Check BIOS memory map for missing/degraded DIMMs
Reseat suspect DIMM(s)
If replacing: match DIMM specs (speed, capacity, rank) to fleet standard
After replacement: verify full memory capacity in BIOS

symptoms.title: Ask Mode Pending / Approval Required
checks: Verify the pending ask-mode request in the management system
Confirm the required approval chain and current status
Document what action is being requested (part swap, firmware update, etc.)
Monitor for approval and proceed with the approved action when granted

symptoms.title: Software / OS Issue
checks: Verify the issue is software-related (not hardware)
Check OS event logs for errors
Verify OS version and patch level
Check for known software issues matching observed symptoms
Escalate to software team if hardware is confirmed healthy

pivotRules.finding: Power / thermal / fatal platform errors in BMC logs
PCIe devices missing (multiple FPGAs not detected)
NIC not enumerating / network adapter missing
NVMe SSD(s) missing from PCIe topology
PXE visible but failing (E16/E18 or timeout)
All devices present � suspect board or CPU issue
BMC recovered after reset � node visible but in D state
Blade appeared in RM after cabling/power fix � now in D state

expectedSideEffects: PXE timeout on first boot is expected (BIOS re-training memory)
NVMe may not appear until second boot cycle
BIOS settings may reset to defaults - verify boot order
Node may power cycle during PSU hot-swap
BMC may log power event - this is expected
Verify PMBus cable routing is correct after reseat
FPGA may take longer to enumerate on first boot after reseat
FpgaDiag should be re-run to verify new card health
If Storm Peak replaced, verify DIMM population (4 per card)
All devices on that riser will re-enumerate
PLX switch may report training errors on first boot - re-check on second boot
NIC link may flap briefly during reseat
Verify cable is not pinched after re-routing
MAC address changes - DHCP lease may need renewal
PXE may fail until switch learns new MAC
Firmware may need update to match fleet standard

actions.description: Minimum boot config performed (1 CPU, 1 DIMM, no FPGAs)
expectedSideEffects: FPGAs will not be detected (intentionally removed)
Reduced memory is expected
This isolates motherboard/CPU - if PXE works here, problem is in removed components
Node will reboot during update
BIOS settings may reset to defaults
First boot after update may be slower (re-training)
All onboard devices get new hardware IDs
BMC/BIOS settings reset to factory defaults
PXE may fail until BIOS boot order is reconfigured
DIMM training on first boot - may take longer
TPM/Cerberus keys change - re-enrollment may be needed
Link may flap during swap
Switch port may need to re-negotiate speed/duplex
If PXE was failing due to bad DAC, it should resolve after replacement
Memory re-training on next boot (may take longer)
Verify full memory capacity in BIOS after boot
BMC may temporarily lose connectivity during reseat
Blade should appear in Rack Manager after reconnection

runbooks.title: FPGA Diagnostic Runbook
steps: Note: output file is available through XTS without JIT permissions
